verify.rule: Ensure all required fields and structures are present.
Ensure the estimated mass does not exceed the allocated budget.

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to insufficient heat sinking.
risk.mit: Increase heat sink size or improve thermal conductivity materials.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference between power and signal lines.
risk.mit: Implement better separation and shielding between power and signal lines.

risk.desc: Difficulty in accessing service paths for maintenance.
risk.mit: Ensure clear and accessible cable exits and service labels.

risk.desc: Inconsistent grounding practices may lead to signal integrity issues.
risk.mit: Standardize grounding schemes and document return paths.

risk.desc: Standoffs may not provide sufficient clearance for all components.
risk.mit: Review standoff placement and clearance requirements.

verify.value: Interfaces are named with exact neighboring subsystem labels and connection details.
verify.rule: Name exact neighboring subsystem labels when they mate, and choose connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature so the neighbor can declare the same joint family back.

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence on critical joints.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

verify.value: Harnesses are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
verify.rule: good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to heat dissipation challenges in confined spaces.
risk.mit: Implement additional cooling fins or channels for improved heat dissipation.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to inadequate strain relief at flex boundaries.
risk.mit: Ensure strain relief geometry is implemented at every cable exit and flex boundary.

risk.desc: Wearable comfort may be compromised if the rigid mock assembly does not account for soft contact cues.
risk.mit: Preserve soft-contact cues and ensure ergonomic clearance in the design.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Flex/bend sensor channels per finger, IMU mount on hand plate

physics_paths.description: Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles.
Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry.
Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals.
